ruleset.name: Error Prone
ruleset.description: Rules to detect constructs that are either broken, extremely confusing or prone to runtime errors.

rule.name: AvoidCallingFinalize
rule.message: Do not call finalize() explicitly; rely on the garbage collector.
rule.description:
Finalize() is called by the garbage collector automatically; calling it explicitly bypasses GC timing and can cause objects to be finalized twice. In Kotlin prefer AutoCloseable / use {} for resource cleanup.
Note: this rule requires kotlin-type-mapper pre-analysis to be available. See the pmd-kotlin documentation for setup instructions.
Kotlin equivalent of Java rule: category/java/errorprone.xml/AvoidCallingFinalize

property.name: javaEquivalentRule
property.description: Java rule this Kotlin rule is based on; update this rule when the Java rule changes.

rule.name: AvoidCatchingGenericException
rule.message: Avoid catching overly broad exception types; catch specific exceptions instead.
rule.description:
Catching Exception or RuntimeException hides real error types and makes error handling imprecise. In Kotlin prefer runCatching {} with .onFailure {}, or catch specific exceptions.
Kotlin equivalent of Java rule: category/java/errorprone.xml/AvoidCatchingGenericException

property.name: javaEquivalentRule
property.description: Java rule this Kotlin rule is based on; update this rule when the Java rule changes.

rule.name: AvoidCatchingThrowable
rule.message: Avoid catching Throwable; it includes Errors that should not be caught.
rule.description:
Throwable is the root of both Exception and Error. Catching it can swallow OutOfMemoryError, StackOverflowError and other JVM errors that are not recoverable. Catch specific exceptions instead. For result-oriented error handling prefer runCatching {} with .onFailure {}, which limits handling to non-fatal exceptions.
Kotlin equivalent of Java rule: category/java/errorprone.xml/AvoidCatchingThrowable

property.name: javaEquivalentRule
property.description: Java rule this Kotlin rule is based on; update this rule when the Java rule changes.

rule.name: AvoidDecimalFormatAsField
rule.message: Avoid using DecimalFormat or ChoiceFormat as a field: they are thread-unsafe.
rule.description:
DecimalFormat and ChoiceFormat are thread-unsafe. Declaring them as fields introduces race conditions. Create a new instance per method call, or use a ThreadLocal.
Note: this rule requires kotlin-type-mapper pre-analysis to be available. See the pmd-kotlin documentation for setup instructions.

rule.name: AvoidDecimalLiteralsInBigDecimalConstructor
rule.message: Avoid creating BigDecimal with a float/double literal; use a String literal instead.
rule.description:
BigDecimal(0.1) does not equal 0.1 exactly due to floating-point representation. Use BigDecimal("0.1"), BigDecimal.valueOf(0.1), or the Kotlin idiomatic "0.1".toBigDecimal() extension for exact decimal values.
Note: this rule requires kotlin-type-mapper pre-analysis to be available. See the pmd-kotlin documentation for setup instructions.
Kotlin equivalent of Java rule: category/java/errorprone.xml/AvoidDecimalLiteralsInBigDecimalConstructor

property.name: javaEquivalentRule
property.description: Java rule this Kotlin rule is based on; update this rule when the Java rule changes.

rule.name: OverrideBothEqualsAndHashcode
rule.message: Ensure you override both equals() and hashCode()
rule.description:
Override both public boolean Object.equals(Object other), and public int Object.hashCode(), or override neither. Even if you are inheriting a hashCode() from a parent class, consider implementing hashCode and explicitly delegating to your superclass.
The idiomatic Kotlin solution is to use a **data class**, which generates correct equals() and hashCode() automatically based on the primary constructor properties.
